description.p: The one was is always invited.

sheet.description: You are born with a disgusting power, with many disgusting possibilities.

description.p: Beginning at 1st level, as an action for 2 cursed energy you create an Insect Swarm in a space you can see within 5 ft of you. You can command the swarm as a bonus action, otherwise it takes the dodge action. Your Insect Swarm is made up of actual insects created from your cursed energy, not shikigami and you can only have one Insect Swarm summoned at a time.

sheet.description: For 2 cursed energy you create an Insect Swarm in a space you can see within 5 ft of you. You can command the swarm as a bonus action, otherwise it takes the dodge action. You can only have one Insect Swarm summoned at a time.

For 2 cursed energy you create an Insect Swarm in a space you can see within 5 ft of you. You can command any number of swarms as a bonus action, otherwise it takes the dodge action. You can have {{proficiency}} Insect Swarm summoned at a time.

description.p: At 3rd level, your mastery over insects has grown. You can have a number of Insect Swarms summoned equal to your proficiency bonus and can command any number of them as a bonus action.

Additionally, you gain access to an insect shikigami with a large pouch. As an action, you can summon up to 2 shikigami, spending 2 cursed energy per shikigami to summon them in an unoccupied space within 10 ft of you. You can command the Shikigami and Insect Swarm as part of the same bonus action.

sheet.description: You can summon up to 2 shikigami, spending 2 cursed energy per shikigami to summon them in an unoccupied space within 10 ft of you. You can command the Shikigami and Insect Swarm as part of the same bonus action.

description.p: At 6th level, you have learned the power of the almighty swarm. As part of the bonus action to command your Insect Swarms, you can order different swarms to move closer and combine into one Vermin Swarm if within 5 ft of each other. The Vermin Swarm gains the combined hit points and damage dice of all the swarms together. The individual Insect Swarms that make up the Vermin Swarms still count against your maximum number of controlled swarms.

sheet.description: As part of the bonus action to command your Insect Swarms, you can order different swarms to and combine into one Vermin Swarm if within 5 ft of each other. The Vermin Swarm gains the combined hit points and damage dice of all the swarms together. The individual Insect Swarms that make up the Vermin Swarms still count against your maximum number of controlled swarms.

description.p: At 11th level, you have learned that your instinctual hunger is not without reason, but exists to stimulate the desire to multiply. As an action for 8 cursed energy, you create an insect egg affixed to a wall/ceiling. The cost to create the egg decreases by 1 CE per creature devoured by you or a swarm in the last 8 hours (min cost 1).

The egg has an AC equal to your Cursed Energy save DC and hit points equal to twice your level. If you die within an hour of creating the egg, it births an identical copy of you from it with current hit points equal to half your hit point maximum. Your cursed energy is passed on after your death, giving your new body your remaining cursed energy from when you died. You do not regain any gear you had beforehand.

sheet.description: For 8 cursed energy, you create an insect egg affixed to a wall/ceiling. The cost to create the egg decreases by 1 CE per creature devoured by you or a swarm in the last 8 hours (min cost 1). The egg has an AC of {{ce:dc:total}} and {{disaster:vermin:parthenogenesis:hp}} hit points. If you die within an hour of creating the egg, it births an identical copy of you from it with {{hp}}/2 current hit points. Your cursed energy is passed on after your death, giving your new body your remaining cursed energy from when you died. You do not regain any gear you had beforehand.

element.name: Domain Expansion: Brood Cathedral of the Crawling God

description.p: Finally at 18th level you have learned to expand your domain. At initiative 20, up to 3 creatures of your choice within your domain take 6d10 necrotic damage as eggs fester and burst from their skin.
A creature that dies within your domain is devoured and becomes an Insect Swarm. This Insect Swarm does not count against your maximum number of controlled swarms until the domain ends.

sheet.description: At initiative 20, up to 3 creatures of your choice within your domain take 6d10 necrotic damage. A creature that dies within your domain is devoured and becomes an Insect Swarm. This Insect Swarm does not count against your maximum number of controlled swarms until the domain ends.

ul.li: Condition Immunities charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned
Insect Crawl. The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.
Bites. Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) piercing damage, or 3 (1d4 + 1) if bloodied.

Blinding Goop. When the shikigami is killed or when you use your reaction to kill it, the shikigami releases a liquid from its sac. Creatures within 5 ft. must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they are blinded for 1 minute. A creature must spend an action to remove the blindness. At 6th level, a creature that fails the save also takes 2d8 acid damage.
Bite. Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

setters.set: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned

description.p: The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

sheet.description: The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

description.p: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

sheet.description: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

description.p: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) piercing damage, or 3 (1d4 + 1) if bloodied.

sheet.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) piercing damage, or 3 (1d4 + 1) if bloodied.

description.p: When the shikigami is killed or when you use your reaction to kill it, the shikigami releases a liquid from its sac. Creatures within 5 ft. must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they are blinded for 1 minute. A creature must spend an action to remove the blindness. At 6th level, a creature that fails the save also takes 2d8 acid damage.

sheet.description: When the shikigami is killed or when you use your reaction to kill it, the shikigami releases a liquid from its sac. Creatures within 5 ft. must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they are blinded for 1 minute. A creature must spend an action to remove the blindness. At 6th level, a creature that fails the save also takes 2d8 acid damage.

description.p: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

sheet.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

element.name: Domain Expansion: Brood Cathedral of the Crawling God Replacement
